title: Process Engineer (Semiconductor Equipment / Skills in Data Analysis & Equipment Design)
description:
Responsibilities

  • Design and implement machine processes to improve machine efficiency, reliability, and product quality.
  • Conduct process corner studies to optimize key parameters and ensure machines operate within defined specifications.
  • Analyze machine performance data to identify trends, improve accuracy, and resolve process-related issues.
  • Develop and validate process control strategies to maintain consistent equipment performance and meet customer specifications.
  • Lead equipment qualification activities (FAT / SAT).
  • Define and document machine technical requirements, including detailed specifications, SOPs, and risk assessments (PFMEA).
  • Perform root cause analysis on equipment and process issues, implementing corrective and preventive actions using methodologies such as 8D and DMAIC.
  • Collaborate with design engineering teams to ensure manufacturability and maintainability of new equipment.
  • Support new product development by scaling prototypes for production readiness.
  • Train manufacturing and customer service personnel on process standards, troubleshooting, and best practices.
  • Evaluate new customer materials and components for compatibility with existing equipment and processes.
  • Provide technical support for customer escalations, including in-house and on-site troubleshooting.
  • Ensure machine compliance with regulatory requirements such as JDEC/EIA standards throughout product development and implementation.
  • Apply statistical process control (SPC) and Six Sigma methodologies to reduce variation and improve machine process capability.
  • Drive continuous improvement projects to enhance equipment performance and reduce operational costs.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering or related discipline.
  • Over 3 years of experience in process or application engineering within semiconductor equipment manufacturing/OEM environment.
  • Strong data analysis skills and using tools such as MS Excel, JMP, or Minitab and methodologies (SPC, Six Sigma, Lean) is required.
  • Strong understanding of semiconductor equipment design, process integration, and manufacturing principles.
  • Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to work cross-functionally with engineering, manufacturing, and customer teams.
  • Programming experience with MS Excel macros or Python is a plus.
  • Six Sigma Green Belt certification or higher is highly desirable.
  • Willingness to work onsite in customer environments approximately 20% of the time.

GMP Technologies (S) Pte Ltd | EA Licence 11C3793 | EA Personnel: Novita Widjaja | Registration No: R22105960

This is in partnership with the Employment and Employability Institute Pte Ltd (“e2i”).

e2i is the empowering network for workers and employers seeking employment and employability solutions. e2i serves as a bridge between workers and employers, connecting with workers to offer job security through job-matching, career guidance and skills upgrading services, and partnering employers to address their manpower needs through recruitment, training, and job redesign solutions. e2i is a tripartite initiative of the National Trades Union Congress set up to support nation-wide manpower and skills upgrading initiatives.

By applying for this role, you consent to GMP Recruitment Services (S) Pte Ltd’s PDPA and e2i’s PDPA.
